name: Ducasse de Mons (Doudou / Lumeçon dragon fight)
annual_date: Trinity Sunday (7 weeks after Easter, May/June)
description: The Ducasse de Mons, also commonly known as the Doudou, is a traditional folk festival held in Mons, Hainaut, Belgium, on Trinity Sunday (57 days after Easter). The feast comprises two important parts: the procession, including taking Saint Waltrude's shrine down from its altar and placing it on a dray, as well as the combat named Lumeçon between Saint George and a dragon. Since 2008, it has been recognised as a Masterpiece of the Oral and Intangible Heritage of Humanity by UNESCO. The Ducasse de Mons or Doudou originates in the Middle Ages. Its origins are difficult to pin down exactly. It has been attested since the 13th century (the first known mention dates from 1248). It is a procession
